name: Uceda and Valdepiélagos – circular from Torrelaguna
name_original: Uceda y Valdepiélagos – circular desde Torrelaguna
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Torrelaguna traverse the Jarama valley within the Sierra Norte of Madrid, offering diverse landscapes. The region features mountainous terrain with ascents through oak and holm oak pastures, alongside routes that follow river valleys and pass significant reservoirs like El Atazar and El Villar. Cyclists can experience both paved and unpaved surfaces, with geological formations adding to the natural interest. Best touring cycling routes around Torrelaguna The most popular touring cycling route is Uceda and Valdepiélagos – circular from Torrelaguna, a 20.3 miles (32.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Jarama valley and surrounding pastures. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is El Villar Reservoir and Gorge – Puerto de la Puebla loop from Torrelaguna, a difficult 70.5 miles (113.4 km) path. This route features substantial climbs and panoramic views of the El Villar Reservoir and the Sierra Norte mountains. Local touring cyclists also love the Patones de Arriba – La Parra Weir loop from Torrelaguna, a 26.1 miles (42.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the historic village of Patones de Arriba, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Touring cycling around Torrelaguna is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and oak and holm oak pastures. The network offers options for moderate to difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Torrelaguna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Torrelaguna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Torrelaguna?
A: Touring cycling routes around Torrelaguna offer a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find both paved and unpaved surfaces, traversing mountainous areas with spectacular oak and holm oak pastures, as well as routes that follow river valleys. Some paths may also feature unique geological formations like "giant kettles" in riverbeds.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in the Torrelaguna area?
A: The routes around Torrelaguna cater to various fitness levels, ranging from moderate to difficult. There are 6 moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962915369/uceda-and-valdepielagos-circular-from-torrelaguna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uceda and Valdepiélagos – circular from Torrelaguna&lt;/a&gt;, and 8 difficult routes, like the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir and Gorge – Puerto de la Puebla loop from Torrelaguna&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available from Torrelaguna?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Torrelaguna are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962915369/uceda-and-valdepielagos-circular-from-torrelaguna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uceda and Valdepiélagos – circular from Torrelaguna&lt;/a&gt;, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23748706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Patones de Arriba – La Parra Weir loop from Torrelaguna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or scenic viewpoints can I discover along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can enjoy panoramic views of the Jarama valley, the Sierra Norte mountains, and significant reservoirs like El Atazar and El Villar. Routes often pass through oak and holm oak pastures, and you might encounter unique geological formations. For specific viewpoints, consider exploring highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5133979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Lozoya River&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6217974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atazar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or cultural points of interest can I visit while cycling near Torrelaguna?
A: Cycling routes often connect Torrelaguna with charming villages and historical sites. Torrelaguna itself is a National Historic and Artistic Heritage Site with its Plaza Mayor and Church of Santa María Magdalena. You can also cycle to places like Patones de Arriba, known for its history and architecture, or encounter ancient watchtowers of Muslim origin, such as the Atalaya El Berrueco.

Q: Are there any gorges or canyons accessible by touring bike in the area?
A: Yes, the area around Torrelaguna features several impressive gorges. You can explore routes that lead to or pass through areas like the Patones Gorge, or visit highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4551346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cárcavas - Pontón de la Oliva&lt;/a&gt;, offering dramatic natural scenery.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Torrelaguna?
A: There are 14 touring cycling routes available around Torrelaguna, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the Jarama valley and the Sierra Norte of Madrid.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Torrelaguna?
A: The touring cycling routes in Torrelaguna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrain to river valleys, and the opportunities to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by significant reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes offer views of or pass by important reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir and Gorge – Puerto de la Puebla loop from Torrelaguna&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, provides panoramic views of the El Villar Reservoir. The region also features the El Atazar Reservoir and the Pontón de la Oliva.

Q: Can I find routes suitable for a longer day trip or multi-day touring?
A: While many routes are suitable for day trips, the region offers options for longer rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir and Gorge – Puerto de la Puebla loop from Torrelaguna&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 113.4 km route, perfect for a long day. The area's network also allows for connecting routes to nearby towns like Miraflores de la Sierra, providing opportunities for extended touring.

Q: What kind of natural monuments can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The Sierra Norte region around Torrelaguna is home to several natural monuments. Cyclists can encounter impressive summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho de la Cabeza Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2786256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondalindo Summit&lt;/a&gt;, or explore mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4182711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Medio Celemín&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique geological and scenic experiences.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Jarama valley?
A: Many routes in the Torrelaguna area traverse the scenic Jarama valley. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962915369/uceda-and-valdepielagos-circular-from-torrelaguna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uceda and Valdepiélagos – circular from Torrelaguna&lt;/a&gt; offers beautiful views of the Jarama valley and its surrounding pastures.
tips: Nice town on this route through the Jarama. Its church stands out, and also the old, Romanesque one, converted into a cemetery center
Formations caused by the erosion of water on the ground.

This municipality has the peculiarity of being the only one in Spain in which primary elections are held prior to the municipal ones. In them, the neighbors who will be part of the Valdepiélagos Independent Candidacy (CIV), which will compete in the mayoral elections, are elected. Each registered neighbor has a ballot in which they can include up to seven names of seven neighbors.
